title: taudit Finding v1
description: Standalone schema for a single taudit Finding object. Published separately from the wrapping report (`taudit-report.schema.json`) and the CloudEvents envelope (`contracts/schemas/taudit-cloudevent-finding-v1.schema.json`) so SIEM teams can validate finding payloads without loading the report wrapper or the CloudEvents envelope. The same Finding shape appears verbatim in three places: as an entry in `findings[]` of the JSON report, as the `data` payload of a `taudit-cloudevent-finding-v1` envelope, and in any external store that round-trips taudit findings (suppression DB, ticket sync, etc.). Schema is `1.x.y` — additive changes only; breaking changes require a `2.0.0` bump.

path.description: Propagation path evidence when the finding involves a chain of nodes (e.g. authority_propagation). Absent for single-node findings.

nodes_involved.description: IDs of authority graph nodes referenced by the finding. Resolves against the same scan's graph; opaque to consumers that only see the Finding.

message.description: Human-readable message. Custom rules prefix with `[<rule_id>] ` so the rule id can be recovered without the schema field. The message is INSENSITIVE for fingerprint computation (operators tweak phrasing without breaking SIEM suppressions).

fingerprint.description: Stable cross-run finding fingerprint (32 lowercase hex chars, SHA-256 truncated to 128 bits). Byte-identical to SARIF `partialFingerprints[primaryLocationLineHash]` and CloudEvents `tauditfindingfingerprint`. Use for SIEM dedup and suppression. Widened from 16 hex (64-bit, v2) to 32 hex (128-bit, v3) in v1.1.0-beta.3 to close a birthday-collision attack against `.taudit-suppressions.yml`. Optional in v1 for backward-compat; always emitted by current taudit. See docs/finding-fingerprint.md.

finding_group_id.description: Optional group identifier when multiple findings share a logical advisory (e.g. authority_propagation hops sharing a root authority). Reserved for the upcoming clustering work; producers MAY omit it. When present, consumers SHOULD treat findings with the same group id as one advisory in summary views.

source.description: Provenance of the rule that emitted this finding: `built-in` (one of the rules shipped with taudit) or an object `{"custom": {"source_file": "<path>"}}` for custom invariants loaded via `--invariants-dir`. Optional in v1 for backward-compat; always emitted by current taudit.

time_to_fix.description: Coarse remediation effort. Triage dashboards sort by `severity * time_to_fix` to surface highest-ROI fixes. Optional. See docs/finding-output-enhancements.md.

compensating_controls.description: Human-readable list of detected controls that already neutralise (or partially neutralise) this finding. Populated when a compensating-control detector downgraded `severity`. Optional.

suppressed.description: True when a matching `.taudit-suppressions.yml` entry was applied in `suppress` mode. The finding still appears (audit trail preserved); consumers may filter on this. Optional.

original_severity.description: Pre-downgrade severity. Populated when the suppression applicator OR a compensating-control detector mutated `severity`. Optional.

suppression_reason.description: Operator-supplied justification from the matching `.taudit-suppressions.yml` entry. Optional.

confidence_scope.description: Evidence boundary for the finding. `yaml_only` means taudit proved the static authority shape in the scanned YAML artifact; runtime/provider settings may still affect exploitability.

runtime_preconditions.description: Runtime or provider-side assumptions to verify before treating the static finding as live exploitability.

portal_control_dependency.description: True when exploitability materially depends on provider-side controls outside YAML, such as Azure DevOps service-connection authorization or GitHub repository settings.

authority_kinds.description: Coarse authority types involved in the finding, such as job_token, oidc_identity, service_connection, variable_group, secret, artifact, or image.

attacker_surface_kinds.description: Coarse attacker-influenced surfaces involved in the finding, such as untrusted_checkout, script_sink, mutable_dependency_ref, reusable_workflow_boundary, or self_hosted_runner.

template_resolution_strength.description: Resolution strength for reusable workflow/template delegation findings.

cve_relationship.description: Relationship between this finding and any cited public CVE/advisory class.

Severity.description: Severity tiers. Order is meaningful: critical > high > medium > low > info. `--severity-threshold` keeps findings at or above the named tier.

FindingCategory.description: Snake_case rule identifier. The list is the union of every `FindingCategory` variant emitted by built-in rules. Custom rules carry their id in the `message` `[<id>] …` prefix and reuse one of these category values for routing.

Recommendation.description: Internally-tagged union (serde `tag = "type"`). The `type` discriminator picks the variant; remaining fields are variant-specific. New variants are added as additive changes within the v1 major.

oneOf.title: tsafe_remediation
oneOf.description: Routes a credential-scope finding to the tsafe vault remediation flow.

oneOf.title: cellos_remediation
oneOf.description: Routes an isolation-scope finding to a CellOS execution-cell spec.

oneOf.title: pin_action
oneOf.description: Replace a floating action ref (`@main`, `@v1`) with a pinned 40-char SHA.

oneOf.title: reduce_permissions
oneOf.description: Narrow a `permissions:` block (or equivalent) from `current` to `minimum`.

oneOf.title: federate_identity
oneOf.description: Replace a long-lived static secret with an OIDC / workload-identity federation.

oneOf.title: manual
oneOf.description: Catch-all when the remediation requires human judgement (e.g. "split this workflow into two").
